title: Erigeron gypsoverus
intro: Erigeron gypsoverus, commonly known as Mountain Fleabane, is a perennial herbaceous plant species native to the mountainous regions of the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antispasmodic effects, making it beneficial for relieving symptoms of digestive issues, such as diarrhea and colitis, as well as soothing respiratory conditions like bronchitis and asthma. The bioactive constituents of Erigeron gypsoverus include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Mountain Fleabane include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to promote digestive health, reduce inflammation, and alleviate respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Erigeron gypsoverus is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. This plant belongs to the genus Erigeron, which includes daisies and fleabanes. The classification of Erigeron gypsoverus is further divided into the kingdom Plantae and the phylum Magnoliophyta. At the level of classification, Erigeron gypsoverus is also known as a dicotyledonous plant. Within the plant kingdom, Erigeron gypsoverus is also classified as an annual or perennial herb.
